name: OpenCode Go
description: OpenAI-compatible chat completions for the OpenCode Go subscription gateway. Flat-rate access to a curated catalog of frontier models (Kimi K2.6, GLM-5.1, Qwen3.6 Plus, DeepSeek V4 Pro, etc.) with multimodal support — text + image inputs in a single request. Use for chat, summarization, structured-output, and image description / captioning where vision-capable models like Kimi K2.6 are bound. One endpoint, one tool, one bearer key.

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: OpenCode Go API Key
credential_fields.description: Create one at https://opencode.ai/account — your subscription bearer token.

tools.name: chat_completion
tools.description: Chat completion via OpenCode Go's OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Supports text-only and multimodal (text + image_url) messages. Returns the assistant's response in OpenAI's standard chat-completion shape. Pick a model from the OpenCode Go catalog (see https://opencode.ai/docs/go/) — recommended: kimi-k2.6 for general chat + vision, glm-5.1 for summarisation, qwen3.6-plus when you want a different style. Vision: pass content as an array of parts where each is either {type:'text', text:...} or {type:'image_url', image_url:{url:...}}. The image URL must be publicly reachable or a data: URI. Tool-calling, structured output (response_format=json_object / json_schema), and streaming are all supported but stream defaults to false for simpler callers.

model.description: Model slug from the OpenCode Go catalog. Common choices: kimi-k2.6 (Moonshot Kimi K2.6, vision-capable, 256K context), kimi-k2.5 (prior generation), glm-5.1 (Zhipu GLM-5.1, 128K context, strong summarisation), qwen3.6-plus (Alibaba Qwen3.6 Plus, 128K), deepseek-v4-pro. Update from https://opencode.ai/docs/go/ as new models ship.

messages.description: Conversation messages in OpenAI chat format. Each item: {role:'system'|'user'|'assistant'|'tool', content: string OR array of content parts}. For multimodal input, content is an array — each part is either {type:'text', text:...} or {type:'image_url', image_url:{url:...}}.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. 0 = deterministic, 1 = balanced, 2 = creative. Lower for summarisation / structured output.

max_tokens.description: Max tokens to generate. The response stops earlier if the model emits an EOS or hits its own limit. Don't set this huge — generation is the slowest part of the call.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling. Leave unset to use the model's default.

stop.description: String or array of strings — model stops when any is generated.

presence_penalty.description: Penalise tokens that have already appeared. -2 to 2. Skip unless you have a reason.

frequency_penalty.description: Penalise tokens by how often they appear. -2 to 2.

tools.description: Tool definitions in OpenAI's tools format. Each: {type:'function', function:{name, description, parameters:<json-schema>}}. Pair with tool_choice to nudge selection.

tool_choice.description: 'auto' (default), 'none', 'required', or {type:'function', function:{name:'...'}} to force a specific tool.

response_format.description: Force JSON output. {type:'json_object'} requires you to instruct JSON in the prompt; {type:'json_schema', json_schema:{name, schema}} validates against a schema. Not all models honour json_schema — check OpenCode Go docs.

seed.description: Best-effort deterministic sampling. Same seed + same prompt → similar output. Not guaranteed.

stream.description: Server-sent events streaming. Defaults to false; integrations that don't speak SSE should leave this off.
